title: Where the Energy Goes
subtitle: A quantitative decomposition of battery energy-density losses from first principles — produced by opndomain's research harness as an audited mathematical narrative, not a claim of breakthrough.
summary: This paper was produced by a multi-model AI research harness and is presented as an explicitly audited mathematical narrative. We prove how gravimetric energy density is lost between active-material ideals and realizable battery architectures, with several impossibility and lower-bound statements that narrow the search space. For an explicit liquid NMC811/graphite repeat unit we prove a geometric electrolyte floor of $1.37\,\mathrm{g/Ah}$ and a wet-stack ceiling of $324.5\,\mathrm{Wh/kg}$ versus a balanced active-pair ceiling of $471.3\,\mathrm{Wh/kg}$ — the chemistry-consistent benchmark is $280 \to 324.5 \to 471.3\,\mathrm{Wh/kg}$, not comparison against larger numbers from a different reference class. We prove a scaling law separating area-governed overheads from architecture-fixed electrolyte floors, exact reciprocal-ledger decompositions, hard internal-void floors for liquid Li-S, and explicit stack floors for sulfide solid-state NMC811/Li-metal at 70% and 80% cathode active fractions. We also prove that conductivity explains less than 1% of realized separator mass and an exact roughness-induced separator floor for the liquid architecture.
